title: Image Generation Flow with Gemini API
code:
graph TD
    Start((Start)) --> Upload[Upload 1-5 Images]
    Upload --> Check{Count > 0?}
    Check -- No --> Error[Show Error]
    Check -- Yes --> Vibe[Select Vibe]
    
    Vibe --> Ratio[Select Aspect Ratio]
    Ratio --> Cook[Click 'Cook It Up']
    
    Cook --> Loading[Show Loading Spinner]
    Loading --> Process[Convert Files to Base64]
    Process --> Prompt[Construct Prompt based on Vibe]
    Prompt --> API[Call Gemini API]
    
    API --> Result{Success?}
    
    Result -- No --> Catch[Catch Error]
    Catch --> DisplayErr[Display Error Message]
    DisplayErr --> Cook
    
    Result -- Yes --> Render[Render Generated Image]
    Render --> Download[Download Image]
    Render --> Retry[Change Vibe/Retry]
    Retry --> Vibe

ai.summary: Flowchart illustrating the process of generating images using the Gemini API, including user input, prompt construction, API calls, and error handling.
ai.whatItShows: This diagram shows the end-to-end process of an image generation application. It starts with user image uploads and selection of a 'vibe' and aspect ratio. The system then converts images to Base64, constructs a prompt, calls the Gemini API, and handles both successful image rendering/download and API failure scenarios with error display and retry options.
ai.whenToUse: Use this diagram when designing an application that leverages AI APIs for content generation, especially image generation. It's suitable for outlining user interaction flows, backend processing steps, and robust error handling for external API integrations.
ai.howToAdapt: This flow can be adapted by integrating different AI models (e.g., DALL-E, Stable Diffusion), adding more complex prompt engineering steps, incorporating user authentication, or expanding error handling to include specific API error codes and user notifications. The 'Vibe' and 'Ratio' steps can be customized for other input parameters.
